original_text:
### Required Elements Within Sections
- Clinical claims require footnote markers (†) with footnote text clearly separated and noticeably smaller (§1.2).
- Patient quotes: attributed as `- [Name], LISRAYA patient`, italic or distinguished styling, paired with warm lifestyle imagery.
- Priovant logo, copyright line, ® legal line, job code [mm/yy], Indication, and ISI all live in the **locked header/footer components — never reproduce in sections.**

quotes: Clinical claims require footnote markers (†) with footnote text clearly separated and noticeably smaller
Clinical claims require footnote markers (†) with footnote text clearly separated and noticeably smaller (§1.2).

rules.intent: Ensure clinical claims are properly qualified and substantiated.
rules.rule_text: Clinical claims must carry footnote markers (†), with the footnote text clearly separated and rendered noticeably smaller (per §1.2, i.e. the footnote type scale, 12px/18px).

quotes: Patient quotes: attributed as `- [Name], LISRAYA patient`, italic or distinguished styling, paired with warm lifestyle imagery.

rules.intent: Present patient testimonials authentically and consistently.
rules.rule_text: Patient quotes must be attributed in the form `- [Name], LISRAYA patient`, rendered in italic or otherwise distinguished styling (patient_story.quote.style), and paired with warm lifestyle imagery (image.photography.treatment = warm realistic patient lifestyle imagery).

effect.subject: Priovant logo, copyright line, ® legal line, job code [mm/yy], Indication, and ISI

quotes: all live in the **locked header/footer components — never reproduce in sections.**
Priovant logo, copyright line, ® legal line, job code [mm/yy], Indication, and ISI all live in the **locked header/footer components — never reproduce in sections.**

rules.intent: Preserve regulatory integrity of locked legal/branding elements.
rules.rule_text: The Priovant logo, copyright line, ® legal line, job code [mm/yy], Indication, and ISI live exclusively in the locked header/footer components and must never be reproduced within content sections.
